is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study genotyped P. falciparum pfcrt gene in children with severe malaria; 'analysed by ... PCR and restriction digest for P. falciparum chloroquine resistance transporter gene (pfcrt) mutations (n=139)'.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfcrt codon 76 (T76/K76) genotypes, a curated target marker position.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1: Pfcrt genotype (T76/K76) 122/17 total, with breakdown by clinical subtype; 88% carried T76 variant (122 of 139).

sub_country_location.evidence: Single site: Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital (KATH) in Kumasi, Ghana — a named town/clinic below country level.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children with severe malaria admitted to a teaching hospital in Kumasi, Ghana.

is_primary_study.evidence: Prospective clinical study of 189 consecutive patients with severe malaria.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site prospective clinical collection at KATH, Kumasi, Nov 2000–Feb 2001. No mention of being part of a larger consortium or shared dataset.

supplement.evidence: Extractable pfcrt T76/K76 counts and single-site location are provided in the main text and Table 1.

assessment.summary: Prospective clinical study of Ghanaian children with severe malaria at Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital, Kumasi (Nov 2000–Feb 2001). Pfcrt codon 76 genotyped in 139 patients: 122 T76 (mutant) / 17 K76 (wild type), with subtype breakdown in Table 1. Meets all criteria: P. falciparum, target marker Pfcrt76, extractable frequencies, sub-country (Kumasi) resolution, <=3y window, African field samples, primary study.
